title: Cheilanthes gracilis
taxonomy: Cheilanthes gracilis is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Cheilanthes, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the lip ferns. This genus is further divided into several species, with Cheilanthes gracilis being one of them. The classification of Cheilanthes gracilis places it within the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. At the highest taxonomic level, it is classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Cheilanthes gracilis is known to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. In some cultures, it is also used to treat digestive problems and as an antiseptic. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cheilanthes gracilis for medicinal purposes.
